themes: model competition (GPT-5.5 imminent, Claude Mythos leak)

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Claude Mythos accidentally leaked on Discord and users accessed it — the same model Anthropic calls too dangerous to release; bad optics on top of the pricing blunder.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: OpenAI's internal model list accidentally leaked inside Codex, with GPT-5.5 release predicted for Thursday — actionable competitive timing signal.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: The most numbers-grounded breakdown of the SpaceX/Cursor $60B option deal, contextualizing what that valuation actually means relative to Cursor's trajectory.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie's high-engagement take that enterprise agent rollout is a massive services and integration opportunity, not just a tooling race — relevant framing for sales enablement.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Karpathy's 1,800x compression claim — that a 1B parameter model on clean data could match today's 1.8T frontier — has direct implications for cost and competitive moat assumptions.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Tight, viral observation: Anthropic went 0→~$350B in 4 years, making employee #200 outcomes historically comparable to being employee #20 at Google — useful distribution/talent framing.

worthClicking.author: @kloss_xyz
worthClicking.why: Clean single-tweet battlefield map of what every lab shipped on the same Tuesday — Anthropic pricing blunder, OpenAI image model, SpaceX/Cursor, Google DESIGN.md — good for orientation.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: OpenAI Codex PMs now show up to meetings with working PRs instead of PRDs — concrete evidence of how the PM role is being redefined at frontier labs.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison confirms the Claude Code pricing change was an A/B test accidentally rolled out globally — resolves the ambiguity around whether it was intentional policy.

voices.why: Consistently first on Anthropic and OpenAI internal leaks with credible specifics — the Mythos and GPT-5.5 scoops this batch are both high-signal.
Produces dense, numbers-first analysis on AI product strategy, PM role evolution, and competitive dynamics; drowns in self-promotional newsletter spam but the actual content is worth filtering for.
Most reliable source for factual resolution of pricing/tooling controversies — confirmed the A/B test explanation here before anyone else.
Short, sharp distribution and competitive moat observations with a founder's lens — the 'single player comprehension bound software' post is a concise thesis worth sitting with.
Enterprise agent adoption framing with real operator credibility; the reply volume on his post signals the take is resonating with a sales-relevant audience.

examples: crypto shill partnership posts (Voran agent infrastructure ad)
RoundtableSpace all-caps content farm recycling prior week's news
aakashgupta newsletter subscription spam posts (5+ identical CTA posts)

emerging.theme: model competition (GPT-5.5 imminent, Claude Mythos leak)

fading.theme: agentic AI at work & BI disruption
